items.title: 1958 Treaty of Peace and cultural normalization
items.description: Formal normalization that enabled cultural and educational exchanges to grow.
items.notes: Frame as reconciliation and foundation for exchange; avoid militaristic or triumphalist language.

items.title: 1974 unrest: lessons in equitable collaboration
items.description: Economic unrest that informs today's emphasis on fairness and partnership.
items.notes: If mentioned, keep focus on mutual learning and transparent collaboration; avoid blame framing.

items.title: Batik x Shibori: textile dialogue
items.description: Workshops and exhibitions blending Indonesian batik and Japanese shibori techniques.
items.notes: Use respectful examples; credit artisans appropriately.

items.title: Wayang meets anime: storytelling workshop
items.description: Youth workshops exploring narrative techniques across traditions.
items.notes: Avoid caricatures; highlight shared values in storytelling.

items.title: Student exchanges and scholarships
items.description: Decades of bilateral study and exchange programs supporting youth.
items.notes: Provide practical benefits; diversify student voices.

items.title: Japanese language learning in Indonesia
items.description: Growth of Nihongo learning communities and cultural clubs.
items.notes: Use inclusive examples; avoid elitist framing.

items.title: Automotive manufacturing collaborations
items.description: Joint ventures supporting skills transfer and jobs in Indonesia.
items.notes: Emphasize fair partnerships and local capacity building.

items.title: SME innovation and startup mentorship
items.description: Workshops linking Indonesian SMEs with Japanese mentors.
items.notes: Highlight mutual learning and concrete outcomes.

items.title: Post-disaster cooperation
items.description: Collaboration on disaster response and reconstruction in Indonesia.
items.notes: Respect affected communities; avoid graphic or sensational content.

items.title: Friendly sports and community ties
items.description: Cultural festivals and friendly tournaments building people-to-people links.
